_comment: Per-soldier combat, Stage C of #32 (cosmetic). Visible in any battle that comes to melee, so this reuses the bundled showcase auto-battle: regiments march in and clash. Two new cosmetic touches build on Stage B's flocking marks. (1) Melee churn: while a regiment is FIGHTING, its front-rank soldier marks press into and recoil from the contact line and jitter sideways, so the engaged edge of the block visibly fights instead of two blocks merely touching; the churn fades a couple of ranks back, so the body of the block holds formation. (2) Fallen markers: each melee/volley casualty drops a small fading heap of dark bodies on the contact edge where the men fell, so you can read where the line is bleeding. Both are purely render-time: mark positions and the body heaps are never fed back into the deterministic aggregate sim (the churn clock is render time, the heap scatter is index-based and never touches the seeded RNG), so combat, morale, balance and replays are all untouched.
caption: Per-soldier combat — #32 Stage C (cosmetic): engaged regiments now churn at the contact line (front-rank men press in and recoil while the rear ranks hold formation), and every hit drops a fading heap of fallen bodies on the contact edge where the men fell. Purely visual — mark motion and the body heaps never feed back into the sim (render-time clock, index-based scatter, no RNG), so combat, morale and replays are unchanged.
